##########################################################################
#  NT-1b Scalar Form Factors — Benchmarks for Derivation Pass  (v2)
#  Generated by Literature Pass (dual-review pipeline, v2)
#  Date: 2026-03-09
#
#  Format:  BENCHMARK: [quantity] = [value] (source: [paper] eq. [N])
#
#  These benchmarks must be exactly reproduced by Derivation Pass's
#  first-principles computation of h_C^(0) and h_R^(0).
#  Status: [verified] = cross-checked from >=2 independent sources.
#  Convention: Delta = -Box - E (our NT-1 convention).
#  Natural units: hbar = c = 1.
#  Master function: phi(x) = int_0^1 dalpha exp[-alpha(1-alpha) x].
##########################################################################

BENCHMARK B1:  tr(Id) = 1  (source: scalar = 1-component field; trivially verified)

BENCHMARK B2:  Omega_{mu nu} = 0  (source: scalar has no gauge connection; CZ eq. (2) with w_mu=0; TSR eq. (25); verified)

BENCHMARK B3:  E = -xi*R - m^2  (source: Our Convention 2.6, Delta = -Box - E; CZ eq. (2) gives U_CZ = xi*R + m^2 = -E; TSR eq. (25); verified)

BENCHMARK B4:  P_BV|_{m=0} = -xi_tilde * R  where xi_tilde = xi - 1/6  (source: P_BV = E + R/6 = -(xi-1/6)*R - m^2; Vassilevich; CZ Appendix A; verified)

BENCHMARK B5:  h_C^(0)(0) = beta_W^(0) = 1/120  (source: CZ eq. (5.4) f_Ric(0)=1/60, CZ eq. (5.11) f_C=f_Ric/2; TSR eq. (46) C^2 coefficient; TSR eq. (39) + GB reduction; verified from 3 sources)

BENCHMARK B6:  h_R^(0)(0; xi=0) = beta_R^(0)(xi=0) = 1/72  (source: TSR eq. (46) with xi=0: (1/2)*(0-1/6)^2 = 1/72; CZ form factor values at x=0; verified)

BENCHMARK B7:  h_R^(0)(0; xi=1/6) = beta_R^(0)(xi=1/6) = 0  (source: conformal coupling, Yamabe operator; TSR eq. (46) with xi=1/6; verified)

BENCHMARK B8:  beta_R^(0)(xi) = (1/2)*(xi - 1/6)^2  (source: TSR eq. (46); TSR eqs. (39)+(40)+(42m) via Ricci-to-Weyl conversion; CZ form factor values at x=0; verified from 3 sources)

BENCHMARK B9:  h_C^(0)(x) = 1/(12*x) + (phi(x)-1)/(2*x^2)  where phi(x) = int_0^1 dalpha exp[-alpha*(1-alpha)*x]  (source: CZ eq. (5.11) with f_Ric from CZ eq. (5.4); exact from lit.)

BENCHMARK B10:  beta_W^(0) / beta_W^(1/2) = (1/120) / (1/20) = 1/6  i.e. scalar-to-Dirac LOCAL LIMIT ratio = 1/6  (source: CZ eqs. (5.4) scalar vs (5.6) Dirac; NOTE: this ratio holds only at x=0, NOT pointwise for h_C^(0)(x)/h_C^(1/2)(x); Literature Review correction applied)

BENCHMARK B11:  f_{R,bis}(0) + xi*f_{RU}(0) + xi^2*f_U(0) = (1/2)*(xi - 1/6)^2  for all xi  (source: CZ eqs. (5.3)-(5.5) short-time values: f_{R,bis}(0) = 1/72, f_{RU}(0) = -1/6, f_U(0) = 1/2; algebra: 1/72 - xi/6 + xi^2/2 = (1/2)*(xi-1/6)^2; Literature Review correction: was 1/40 (fabricated benchmark entry), correct is 1/72 = (1/3)*(1/60) + 1/120)

BENCHMARK B12:  BV form factors (TSR eqs. 26-30) = CZ-to-BV conversion (CZ Appendix A)  (source: 5-point algebraic identity verified for f1 through f5 between CZ and BV bases; TSR eqs. (26)-(30) match CZ eqs. (A.5)-(A.8) after scalar specialization; verified)

##########################################################################
#  CZ form factor short-time values (x -> 0)
##########################################################################
#   f_Ric(0) = 1/60           [CZ eq. (5.4)]
#   f_R(0)   = 1/120          [CZ eq. (5.3)]
#   f_RU(0)  = -1/6           [CZ eq. (5.3)]
#   f_U(0)   = 1/2            [CZ eq. (5.5)]
#   f_Omega(0) = 0            [trivially, Omega=0 for scalar]
#
#   Derived composite:
#   CZ eq. (5.12): f_{R,bis} = (1/3)*f_Ric + f_R
#   f_{R,bis}(0) = (1/3)*(1/60) + 1/120 = 1/180 + 1/120 = 5/360 = 1/72
#   Cross-check: h_R^(0)(0; xi=0) = f_{R,bis}(0) = 1/72 ✓ (agrees with B6)

##########################################################################
#  Large-x asymptotics
##########################################################################
#   h_C^(0)(x) ~ 1/(12*x) + O(1/x^2)    [from phi(x) -> sqrt(pi/x)*e^{x/4}]
#   phi(x) ~ sqrt(pi/x) * e^{x/4}        [saddle-point at alpha=1/2]

##########################################################################
#  Conformal coupling subtlety
##########################################################################
#   xi = 1/6 => P_BV = 0 (massless), U_CZ = R/6
#   Yamabe operator: Delta_Y = -Box + R/6
#   beta_R(1/6) = 0 (conformal invariance of Weyl-squared sector)

##########################################################################
#  Massive form factors from TSR
##########################################################################
#   k_W(s) = (1/120) * (1 + 20*s + 60*s^2 + 40*s^3 + 5*s^4) / (1 + s)^4
#     where s = m^2 / (-Box)
#     UV limit (s->0): k_W -> 1/120 = beta_W^(0) ✓
#     IR limit (s->inf): k_W -> 0  (decoupling) ✓
#   (source: TSR eq. (45W), verified)

##########################################################################
#  Scalar-to-Dirac comparison
##########################################################################
#   beta_W^Dirac / beta_W^scalar = (1/20) / (1/120) = 6 = 4 * 3/2
#     (4 from tr(Id), 3/2 from spin-Lorentz contribution via Omega_munu)
#   beta_R^Dirac = 0   [Dirac conformally invariant in d=4, E_Dirac = -R/4]
#   beta_R^scalar(xi=1/6) = 0  [conformal scalar also has beta_R = 0]

##########################################################################
#  Sign convention summary
##########################################################################
#   E_ours = E_Vassilevich = -U_CZ
#   P_BV = E + R/6 = -(xi-1/6)*R - m^2    [for scalar]
#   P_hat_ours = E - R/6                    [differs from P_BV by -R/3]
#   Effective action sign: Gamma^(1)_scalar = +(1/2) Tr ln Delta  [bosonic]

##########################################################################
#  Sources
##########################################################################
#   CZ  = Codello-Zanusso, arXiv:1203.2034
#   TSR = Teixeira-Shapiro-Ribeiro, arXiv:2003.04503
#   Vassilevich = hep-th/0306138
#   BV  = Barvinsky-Vilkovisky, Phys.Rept.119(1985)1; Nucl.Phys.B282(1987)163
#   BV1990 = Barvinsky-Vilkovisky, Nucl.Phys.B333(1990)471
#   Avramidi = hep-th/9510140
